justification: MJ produced a full architecture design with microservices, event-driven patterns, CQRS, sagas, component diagrams, domain models, trade-offs, implementation guidance, and a technology stack — all from a vague prompt with zero NFRs. Instead of refusing to design and escalating with specific NFR questions, MJ invented assumptions (single region, <100K orders/day, three user types) and designed against them. Every failure mode in the rubric was triggered: architecture pattern recommended, NFRs invented, trade_offs section produced, lengthy document produced from an empty brief.

observations.text: Produced a complete microservices/event-driven architecture with 8 components, detailed interfaces, and data flow diagrams — should have refused to design without NFRs
Invented NFRs in the Assumptions section: 'single geographic region', '<100K/day at launch', 'three distinct user types', 'payment provider is external' — exactly the anti-pattern the rubric flags
Produced a full trade-offs table with 5 detailed entries comparing architectural decisions — no design should exist to trade off against
Recommended specific patterns (choreographed saga, CQRS, pub/sub, strangler fig) without any load, latency, or consistency requirements to justify them
Included a technology stack (Kafka, Redis, PostgreSQL, Kong, Socket.IO) and module structure — pure speculation without knowing team size, operational maturity, or scale requirements
Response is extremely lengthy (~3000 words) when the rubric requires concise escalation only
No escalation to request missing NFRs — zero questions asked about expected volume, latency, consistency, team size, geographic scale, or availability requirements

agent_output_excerpt:
# Food Delivery Platform — Architecture Design

## Executive Summary

A food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers is a classic distributed systems challenge with real-time coordination at its core. The architecture uses an event-driven microservices approach organized around four bounded contexts (Ordering, Restaurant, Delivery, Identity), with a real-time event bus as the nervous system. This design optimizes for independent team scalability, real-time respo

forbidden values found: microservices, event-driven, trade_off
